danger_signals.visual_form: NPC entities visible on tactical screen when spotted by player-controlled or allied characters
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates hostile presence; triggers transition from real-time to turn-based combat

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual transition and game mode switch when player and enemy forces occupy same sector
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent tactical engagement requiring immediate defensive/offensive response

danger_signals.visual_form: Mercenary character state indicating health damage requiring medical attention
danger_signals.meaning: Reduced combat effectiveness and need for medical kit intervention

danger_signals.visual_form: Weapon, tool, and armor degradation status visible in inventory/equipment UI
danger_signals.meaning: Reduced functionality requiring mechanical skill repair before deployment

danger_signals.visual_form: Strategic map display showing sector occupation by enemy forces (grid-based sector system)
danger_signals.meaning: Contested territory requiring military reclamation to progress campaign

safety_signals.visual_form: Sector ownership indicator on strategic map screen
safety_signals.meaning: Secure territory controlled by player; militia can be stationed for defense

safety_signals.visual_form: Mercenary movement option toggle allowing silent, noise-free traversal
safety_signals.meaning: Reduced enemy detection risk; tactical advantage for infiltration

safety_signals.visual_form: Mercenary with medical kit and medical skill assigned to tend wounded mercs
safety_signals.meaning: Recovery acceleration and restoration of combat effectiveness

safety_signals.visual_form: Local citizens trained into militia status by trainer mercs in controlled sectors
safety_signals.meaning: Passive sector defense capability allowing mercenary deployment elsewhere

safety_signals.visual_form: Ground or aerial vehicle availability for troop transport on strategic map
safety_signals.meaning: Accelerated sector traversal reducing exposure time during movement

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct from enemy forces through character models and allegiance state
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sector ownership display on strategic map; militia presence in controlled towns

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy NPC entities occupying contested and controlled sectors
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sector occupation on strategic grid; enemy troop deployment across Arulco

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Trained citizens can be distinguished from mercenaries through training status
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence in towns after training; assigned to defend specific sectors passively

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Strategic map sector control distribution; number of enemy-held vs. player-controlled territories
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy force deployment patterns; increasing difficulty and enemy presence as player advances

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mercenary roster size and equipment quality; weapon/armor upgrades displayed in inventory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat difficulty scaling based on hired mercenary experience and tactical loadout

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Real-time grid-based sector ownership changes on strategic map after successful military action
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy force reallocation and reinforcement patterns in neighboring sectors

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Militia training indicators in player-controlled sectors; trained citizen availability count
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Passive sector defense by militia when player forces are deployed elsewhere

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Health status of individual mercs; equipment condition (damaged/functional); skill training progress
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Action point allocation per turn affected by physical state; medical recovery time visible in strategic screen

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Isometric tactical screen with terrain, buildings, items; character positioning relative to cover
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game mode transition from real-time to turn-based upon enemy spotting; interrupt chance display

environmental_storytelling.example: Strategic map displays square grid of Arulco sectors with ownership status, conveying territorial conflict without narrative exposition
Tactical isometric view allows discovery of items, destructible obstacles, and cover positions indicating how towns have been fortified under Deidranna's rule
Pattern of enemy force placement on strategic map communicates Deidranna's military strategy and territorial priorities
Availability of local citizens for militia training indicates degree of local resistance and support for player campaign against Deidranna

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Triggers automatic game mode switch to turn-based combat; position locked in tactical view
Attempts noise-free movement; reduced enemy detection probability
Reduced action point allocation per turn; requires medical attention to restore combat capability
Stationary position in sector; increases local citizen skill progression for militia development
Invisible on tactical screen until player-controlled character achieves line of sight
Participates in turn-based action economy; spends action points for movement, attacks, and tactical maneuvers
Provides passive sector defense when stationed; independent defensive capability without player direct control

notes: Jagged Alliance 2 uses a dual-layer visual communication system: (1) Strategic map with grid-based sector ownership for high-level world state, and (2) Isometric tactical screen for granular combat and environmental detail. The game lacks explicit color-coded faction identity systems, instead relying on NPC entity differentiation, character roster management, and sector control visualization. World state is primarily communicated through strategic map configuration, mercenary equipment/health status, and the presence/absence of enemy forces in specific sectors. Political situation (Deidranna's grip on Arulco) is expressed through the distribution of enemy-controlled territories rather than explicit faction visual markers.
